pages.title: About - Elsie Blundell MP
pages.meta_description: Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North
pages.text_excerpt: About - Elsie Blundell MP Skip to main content Elsie Blundell MP Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North About Contact Campaigns End Furniture Poverty Royal Mail Bring Back the Express! World Book Day Fly High More Menu About Portrait of Elsie Blundell MP - ©House of Commons/Roger Harris I was born in Barrow, Cumbria, attending school and growing up there until attending the University of Manchester to study politics and international relations, graduating in 2012. I moved to Rochdale in 2015 and have been active in the community and on the local council. I have come to love it here, building my life and starting a family locally. I am passionate about the area and the importance of local people having as much opportunity to live happily, work, and start a family as anywhere else. That’s why I will stand up for Heywood and Middleton North, including the communities of Castleton, Bamford, Spotland, Falinge, and Norden, in every way I can. On the 30th September 2024, myself and my husband, John, celebrated the birth of our first child. Elsie Blundell MP Contact details Email: elsie.blundell.mp@parliament.uk Further details Elsie Blundell MP 15 Bridge Street Heywood OL10 1JB ©2026 Copyright Elsie Blundell MP. All rights reserved. headings: h2: Campaigning for Heywood and Middleton North
h2: Open Letter to Andy Burnham
pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns - Elsie Blundell MP Skip to main content Elsie Blundell MP Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North About Contact Campaigns End Furniture Poverty Royal Mail Bring Back the Express! World Book Day Fly High More Menu Campaigns On my election I promised to stand up for the wonderful people and places of Heywood and Middleton. I want to see opportunities, jobs and a better quality of life brought to our area. We will do this with better infrastructure like Transport and internet connection. We deserve better schools, and Early Years support for parents and children alike. And we should have strong, sustainable communities which protect and serve citizens. I will champion our area and put my energy and passion into improving our communities. I hope you will join me. Email me if you have any ideas or want to participate: elsie.blundell.mp@parliament.uk Campaigning for Heywood and Middleton North See more Open Letter to Andy Burnham Bring Back the 167 - an express direct-to-Manchester city centre bus service. bus Campaigns Bring Back the 167! pages.title: Bring Back the Express! - Elsie Blundell MP
pages.meta_description: Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North
pages.text_excerpt: Bring Back the Express! - Elsie Blundell MP Skip to main content Elsie Blundell MP Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North About Contact Campaigns End Furniture Poverty Royal Mail Bring Back the Express! World Book Day Fly High More Menu Bring Back the Express! Bring Back the Express! Will you join me in calling for an express direct-to-Manchester city centre bus service to be reinstated? The service could be an employment life line, an affordable option to access the jobs and opportunities within the Greater Manchester city region, one of the most dynamic economies in the country. I am sending an open letter to Mayor Andy Burnham, calling on him to Bring Back the Express! Read the letter and sign it below. Dear Mayor Andy Burnham, As a resident in Heywood and Middleton North, I want to see a fast and reliable cheap public transport solution to my problem of getting into Manchester City Centre. For work, leisure, education, or health reasons, many people rely on Manchester as the centre of our city region. But not every area can reach the City Centre easily. In fact some areas are being left behind – unable to access the jobs and opportunities that exist. I live in one of those areas. Elsie Blundell MP has been campaigning to Bring Back the Express – an express, direct to Manchester city centre service, serving the areas in her constituency. Her proposal is backed by a survey that shows such a service would be popular with local people and increase their willingness to use the buses. I am adding my name to this open letter as a resident of the area and ask you to Bring Back the Express! Signed, Resident of Heywood and Middleton North via Elsie Blundell MP Bring Back the Express! Sign my open letter to Mayor Andy Burnham and join the community calling for an express direct-to-Manchester city centre bus service to be reinstated. Name (Required) First Last Email (Required) Enter Email Confirm Email I live in... (Required) Bamford Castleton Heywood Middleton Norden Spotland I don't live in Heywood and Middleton North Other I use the bus.... (Required) Often Sometimes Hardly ever Never I would use the bus more if there was a fast option to Manchester City Centre... Yes No I would use an express bus service to Manchester for... (tick all that apply) Work Leisure To attend health facilities To visit friends and family Select All Consent (Required) Thank you! I will add your name to the Open Letter to Mayor Andy Burnham asking him to Bring Back the Express! By submitting you consent to receive communications from me and my office in my role as Member of Parliament and to update you on Mayor Burnham's response. I will send updates and occasional surveys to you, using the contact details above to help me reflect the views and opinions of my constituents in Heywood and Middleton North. Responses in all my surveys are kept confidential. If you want me to respond to you personally about a particular issue, please email me and my casework team: elsie.blundell.mp@parliament.uk I agree to the privacy policy. pages.title: Towns, Economy, Housing and Jobs – Questions at Transport Select Committee - Elsie Blundell MP
pages.h1: Towns, Economy, Housing and Jobs – Questions at Transport Select Committee
pages.meta_description: Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North
pages.text_excerpt: Towns, Economy, Housing and Jobs – Questions at Transport Select Committee - Elsie Blundell MP Skip to main content Elsie Blundell MP Parliamentary Website for Heywood and Middleton North About Contact Campaigns End Furniture Poverty Royal Mail Bring Back the Express! World Book Day Fly High More Menu Towns, Economy, Housing and Jobs – Questions at Transport Select Committee Heidi Alexander is the Secretary of State for Transport, and I had an opportunity to ask questions about some important strategic topics which affect Heywood and Middleton North at the Transport Select Committee in April 2025. I believe that better transport links are vital for towns like Heywood and Middleton. Investment and strategic decision-making needs to be bold and make the most of new opportunities like Great British Rail (the new state-owned rail operator). Only a big commitment will make a transformational difference in areas that have been underserved for transport links in the future. We were all very appreciative of Heidi to come and appear at the Select Committee and answering our questions. It’s important that Government ministers come and talk to us, and I send my thanks. Elsie Blundell MP Contact details Email: elsie.blundell.mp@parliament.uk Further details Elsie Blundell MP 15 Bridge Street Heywood OL10 1JB ©2026 Copyright Elsie Blundell MP. All rights reserved.
